codeReview.summary: The reviewed diff correctly adds `specs/NativeBiometricVault.ts` with the exact five-method Turbo Module surface required by the contract, mirrors the existing spec style, and registers `NativeBiometricVault` -> `RCTNativeBiometricVault` in `codegenConfig.ios.modulesProvider` without disturbing the existing native-module entries or Android package name. I found no blocking or non-blocking code defect in the implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `VAL-NATIVE-031` is assigned to this spec-only feature, but the assertion's evidence spans later-delivered native/mock files outside the reviewed commit. That makes the feature-to-assertion mapping ambiguous even though the current branch now uses the `NativeBiometricVault` name consistently everywhere.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `validation-contract.md:1100-1114` requires the `NativeBiometricVault` literal across `specs/NativeBiometricVault.ts`, `ios/EnboxMobile/NativeBiometricVault/RCTNativeBiometricVault.mm`, `android/app/src/main/java/org/enbox/mobile/nativemodules/NativeBiometricVaultModule.kt`, `package.json`, and `jest.setup.js`; `git show --name-only 6115c62bf678360b259c5f3bb2040b2e56082d36` shows this feature only changed `specs/NativeBiometricVault.ts` and `package.json`.

summary: Pass: the feature cleanly implements the biometric-vault Turbo Module spec and iOS codegen registration with the expected signatures and module name. No code bugs found; the only follow-up is a shared-state assertion-mapping gap because `VAL-NATIVE-031` depends on files introduced by later features.
